title: pinus ustulata
intro: Pinus ustulata, also known as the rough-bark pine, is a species of pine tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This evergreen tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of terpenes and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Pinus ustulata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Pinus ustulata can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and make it available for consumption.
